prompt:
Scene:
A square cooking-hack thumbnail for mobile feeds, using a split composition: sizzling pan close-up on the right and expressive chef face on the left. The background is warm cream with orange and green accents, plus a clear top category bar and a bold empty hook block overlapping the pan edge. The overall look should be bright, simple, and instantly edible.

Subject:
A charismatic home-cook creator in an apron reacts with delight while lifting chopsticks above a pan where steam and one dramatic cheese or sauce pull creates a single irresistible food cue. The ingredients should stay large and recognizable: one pan, one bright garnish, one utensil. The right-side hook area uses non-readable blocks and one badge-like circle to imply the platform template.

Important details:
Thumbnail logic comes first. Keep the cook's face large and expressive, keep the food cue singular and obvious, and avoid restaurant-menu clutter. Use strong warm contrast so the frame survives downscaling. This should look like a repeatable short-video cover system for kitchen hacks rather than a plated-food advertisement.

Use case:
Short Video Cover Pack single study 7/9, focused on split-screen cooking-cover logic with face-plus-food clarity.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no crowded ingredient spread, no multiple dishes, no greasy mess, no fine-print labels or recipe cards.
title: Kitchen Hack Sizzle
description: A warm cooking thumbnail pairing one expressive creator face with one clear sizzling food cue and a strong hook block.

generation.runner_notes: Independent cooking-thumbnail study emphasizing split composition and appetizing clarity.
